result.question: Research to enrich a mortgage-calculator SEO/AEO content silo (site: mortgages.plus). Need actionable, current (2026) findings for these calculator keywords and their pages: Mortgage Payment, Monthly Mortgage, Mortgage Interest, 30-Year Mortgage, Closing Cost, Mortgage Amortization, Mortgage Payoff, Early Mortgage Payoff, Mortgage Affordability, Mortgage Refinance, Mortgage Rates, Rent vs Buy, Debt-to-Income, Reverse Mortgage, Commercial Mortgage, FHA Mortgage. For each: (1) typical search intent + common "People Also Ask"/FAQ questions, (2) the standard inputs and outputs a best-in-class version of that calculator should include, (3) current 2026 reference figures where relevant — e.g. 2026 conforming loan limit, 2026 FHA loan limits + upfront/annual MIP rates, current HECM/reverse-mortgage max claim (lending) limit and principal-limit-factor basics, typical closing-cost percentages, DTI thresholds (FHA/conventional/QM 43%), typical commercial mortgage terms (amortization vs balloon, DSCR). Also: SEO + Answer Engine Optimization best practices for a mortgage-calculator silo (internal linking, schema/structured data, content depth, E-E-A-T for YMYL finance pages). Provide citations.

findings.claim: Confirmed 2026 figures for mortgages.plus (11 claims merged). Conforming one-unit limit $832,750 (+3.26%); high-cost ceiling $1,249,125 (FHFA). FHA one-unit floor $541,287, ceiling $1,249,125, eff Jan 1 2026 (HUD). FHA UFMIP 1.75% of base loan; MIP full term under 10% down, ends 11yr if 10%+. HECM max claim $1,249,125 (+$39,375), eff Jan 1 2026; HECM most common reverse mtg, FHA-insured. Reverse principal limit = max claim x PLF (youngest age + expected rate, 5% floor); max claim = lesser of value/sale/HUD limit; ex age70/5%/PLF0.465/$300k=$139,500 (2-1). FHA DTI 31/43 guideline, up to ~47/50 with compensating factors. PMI (conventional) under 20% down ~0.46-1.50%/yr. Best calc: price, down payment $/%, term 30/20/15/10, rate + optional taxes/insurance/HOA/extra; outputs PITI+PMI/MIP.
findings.evidence: 10/11 passed 3-0 vs FHFA/HUD/CFPB/24CFR206.3 + industry; PLF example and 31% front 2-1 secondary. Arithmetic verified.

result.summary: 2026 figures confirmed for mortgages.plus: conforming $832,750/ceiling $1,249,125 (FHFA); FHA floor $541,287/ceiling $1,249,125 (HUD); HECM max claim $1,249,125 eff Jan 1 2026; FHA UFMIP 1.75%, MIP full-term under 10% vs 11yr at 10%+; DTI 31/43 up to 47/50; PMI under 20% down ~0.46-1.50%/yr. Reverse principal-limit math and best-in-class calculator I/O documented. Gap: 2026 FHA annual MIP schedule unverified.
result.caveats: 2026 figures expire when 2027 limits announced; date-stamp pages. 2026 FHA annual MIP schedule NOT confirmed (blog 0.55/0.50% and an FHA floor/ceiling claim refuted 1-2 and 0-3); re-verify vs HUD Mortgagee Letter. 43% FHA back-end DTI as hard cap refuted (0-3): guideline/QM only. PLF example and 31% front 2-1 secondary; illustrative. PLF tables post-Oct-2017 presumed current. HECM authority is HUD ML 2025-22 (aka HUD No.25-145).

PAA/FAQ + FAQ-schema per keyword (Rent vs Buy, Refinance, Early Payoff)?

refuted.claim: For standard FHA loans (loan amount <= $524,225) with 30-year terms, the 2026 annual MIP is 0.55% for less than 5% down and 0.50% for 5-10% down.
The 2026 FHA baseline (floor) loan limit is $524,225 and the high-cost area ceiling is $1,209,750.

HECM principal limit factors (PLF) increase with borrower age, ranging roughly from 37.6% at age 62 to 50.5% at age 80, representing the percentage of the maximum claim amount available as loan proceeds.

The HECM principal limit is computed from the maximum claim amount, the youngest borrower's age, and the expected interest rate (EIR); e.g., a $850,000 max claim amount with a borrower age of 75 and 6.00% EIR yields a PLF of 0.4819 and a principal limit of $408,850.
For 2026, FHA loans limit the back-end (total debt) DTI ratio to 43% of gross monthly income.
